prerequisites: A Granola account on a Business or Enterprise plan, the minimum plan required to create API keys.
Access to the Granola desktop application, where API keys are created under Settings.
On Enterprise plans, a workspace admin must first enable API access scopes for members in Settings > Workspace > General.
A generated Granola API key (format grn_...) with the desired note access scope for the api_key field.
Optionally, a start_date value to limit the time range of notes ingested.

authMethods.name: API key (Bearer token)
authMethods.summary: The Granola API uses Bearer token authentication. Supply your API key (format grn_...) in the connector's api_key field; requests are authenticated with the header Authorization: Bearer grn_YOUR_API_KEY against https://public-api.granola.ai/v1/.

setupSteps.title: Confirm plan and enable API access
setupSteps.body: Ensure your workspace is on a Business or Enterprise plan, the minimum required to create API keys. On Enterprise plans, have a workspace admin enable API access scopes for members under Settings > Workspace > General before continuing.

setupSteps.title: Create an API key in the Granola desktop app
setupSteps.body: Open the Granola desktop application and go to Settings > Connectors > API keys. Select Create new key, choose the note access scope you need (personal or public notes), then generate the key. Copy the generated key, which begins with the grn_ prefix.

setupSteps.title: Provide the API key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Paste the generated key into the connector's required api_key field. This value is used as the Bearer token for all requests to the Granola API.

setupSteps.title: Optionally set the start date
setupSteps.body: If you want to limit ingestion to notes from a specific point onward, set the optional start_date config field. Leave it unset to use the connector default.
